summary: `mentionScope` and `SEAM_VERSION` (sdk/src/declarations.ts) are exported with zero callers anywhere in sdk/src or sdk/test — each used only inside its own defining file

edit.description: Drop the `export` keyword from `mentionScope` (849, called only internally at 884's `nestedMemberRows` call) and from `SEAM_VERSION` (891, read only internally at 900's `encodeSeam`); `rg 'mentionScope|SEAM_VERSION' sdk/src sdk/test` (excluding declarations.ts's own definitions and doc-comment @link mentions) finds no import or call site anywhere else. Both stay module-private; no behavior change. Addresses re-read and shifted -24 by DECLARATIONS-TELEMETRY-HOOK-PROVIDER-FACE-MOVE (a6db2b5), which removed the TELEMETRY_EVENT_HOOKS block ahead of this region; confirmed at HEAD (4ab6fe2).

acceptance: `export` is gone from `mentionScope` and `SEAM_VERSION` in sdk/src/declarations.ts; `rg 'export function mentionScope|export const SEAM_VERSION' sdk/src/declarations.ts` finds nothing. `pnpm --dir sdk test` green, unchanged behavior.
notes: Zero-consumer, grep-verified over sdk/src + sdk/test excluding declarations.ts's own definitions. SDK root also excludes both from its public surface (sdk/test/emit.test.ts:683 asserts SEAM_VERSION/compileDeclarations absent). Blocker DECLARATIONS-TELEMETRY-HOOK-PROVIDER-FACE-MOVE shipped (19258b7/a6db2b5), verified clean — entry unblocked; cited lines rescoped -24 for that move's shift. scoped at 4ab6fe2.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (75) sits under a doc (71-74) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (650) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (677), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 548 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 647-648 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 672-675 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). f6731fb (DIRECTIVE_FIELD consolidated into graph.rs ahead of this region, in the d1af9a5..HEAD window) shifted every address below its insertion point; re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff), so every address above now carries at its new line.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1547) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1548-1552) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1550) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. Addresses re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff) after upstream graph.rs/test-file shifts; they carry at these lines.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at ac40c72 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. f6731fb shifted graph.rs's const region from 59/55-58 to 75/71-74 (DIRECTIVE_FIELD moved in ahead of it); value and cite date unchanged (5, 2026-07-02). `files[]` line cites rewritten to match. The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 0522eff.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at ac40c72, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff a23269d..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.

summary: Add a predicate-less `guidance` channel to `kind()` and to field declarations (decision 0045), serialized into the lock as a new kind/field-attached fact distinct from a clause's guidance column

edit.description: `KindOptions` (319-321) gains an advisory `guidance?: string` (kind-level authoring counsel) and a new field-level slot — e.g. `fieldGuidance?: readonly { field: string; guidance: string; cite?: string }[]` — composed the way `clause()` already composes a predicate's severity/guidance/cite (contract.ts:310-315). Both erase before `kind()`'s `construct` (330-343) builds a member, riding on the returned `KindDefinition` the way `options.render` already does, never on the member itself.
`kindFactRow()` (294) serializes the new kind-level `guidance` and the field-guidance list into the row it returns, alongside the five facts it already lifts.

`KindFactRow` (3045) gains an optional `guidance: Option<String>` column plus a new optional field-guidance row list (its own struct: field name + guidance + optional cite), the same tolerant `#[serde(default)]` absent/empty round-trip `templates`/`registration` already take. `to_table`/`from_table` (3953-) extend to match. The generated TS bindings under `sdk/src/generated/` regenerate from these structs via the project's existing ts-rs export step.

`from_kind_fact_row` (577) lifts both new columns into the reconstructed `CustomKind` alongside the facts it already lifts (588-601) — a name-keyed guidance-string list, not the per-kind `Field` primitive the file's own comment (556-566) says the engine deliberately holds none of.

schemaDelta: New optional `guidance` column on KindFactRow (kind-level) plus a new optional field-guidance row list (field name -> guidance string, optional cite, no predicate/severity).

tests.asserts: a kind() carrying `guidance` and a field carrying `fieldGuidance` round-trip unchanged through emit into their lock rows
KindFactRow round-trips byte-identically when the new guidance columns are absent, and reads back a present kind/field guidance unchanged

acceptance: kind() and field declarations carry an optional, predicate-less `guidance` (kind-level) / field-guidance list (field-level); both serialize into the lock. `pnpm --dir sdk test` and `cargo test` green.
notes: First of three (decision 0045's SDK/engine-deliver/promote-notes split, inbox-routed). blockedBy DECLARATIONS-ZERO-CONSUMER-EXPORTS-PRUNE — both edit sdk/src/declarations.ts (pending-entry rule, 'Disjoint, or serialized'), that entry sized smaller and already pickable. No existing field-declaration primitive to extend, so this introduces the first one, scoped to name+guidance+cite, never a field type/shape registry. scoped at 946e303.

summary: `temper schema` renders kind/field guidance as JSON Schema hover description and `temper explain` narrates it — authoring counsel delivered at authoring time, never a check (decision 0045)

edit.description: `emit()` (51) merges a kind's own guidance into the schema's top-level `description`, and a field's no-predicate guidance into that field's property `description` (the docs channel described at 9-25), joined alongside any clause guidance already riding that property the same way a field's several guided clauses already join into one description.

`explain` (240) narrates a member; extend it to surface an attached kind/field guidance alongside its existing narration — evidence narrated, never judged, the same posture `contract.md`'s 'Read verbs' already states for the rest of `explain`.

tests.asserts: a kind()-level guidance with zero clauses at all still produces a schema carrying a top-level `description`; a field's guidance with zero clauses on that field still gets a property `description`
explain's output includes a kind/field's attached guidance text

acceptance: A kind/field carrying guidance surfaces it through `schema` and `explain`; a guidance-only kind or field never fails `check`, pinned by a test asserting an otherwise-empty contract still passes. `cargo test` green.
notes: Second of three; blockedBy the SDK/lock entry since it needs KindFactRow's new columns to read. The docs-channel split (schema.rs:9-25) already keeps guidance out of validation keywords — this only widens which guidance sources feed that one existing channel, never a new keyword. scoped at 946e303.

summary: Promote the built-ins' stranded JSDoc 'Authoring notes the clauses cannot carry' (plugin-manifest, marketplace, skill) into the new guidance channel, cut from the doc comment once moved

edit.description: Three stranded blocks move from doc-comment prose into the new guidance channel at their kind's own `kind()`/contract call: `pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s `version`/`defaultEnabled` note (705-707), `marketplaceDefaultContract`'s `source`-resolution/`defaultEnabled`-precedence note (1049-1052), and `skillDefaultContract`'s naming/versioning note (1279-1284). Each doc comment is cut once its content is promoted — `rg 'Authoring notes the clauses cannot carry' sdk/src/builtins.ts` finds zero left.

tests.asserts: each promoted note is reachable through the moved kind/field's guidance (schema hover / explain), the same way the entry before this one pinned delivery

acceptance: `rg 'Authoring notes the clauses cannot carry' sdk/src/builtins.ts` finds nothing; every promoted note surfaces through `schema`/`explain` on its kind or field. `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Third of three, blockedBy the delivery entry — moving prose into a channel that doesn't deliver yet would strand it worse (invisible in a doc comment AND absent from schema/explain). scoped at 946e303.
